word_list.word: it is not over (till|until) it is (actually|) over
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz nˌɑːt ˌoʊvɚ tˈɪl ʌntˈɪl ɪt ɪz ˈæktʃuːəli ˈoʊvɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that one should not prematurely assume the outcome of a situation, but rather remain persistent and open-minded, as there may still be opportunities for success

examples.x: When Sarah's business was struggling and she felt like giving up, she reminded herself that it is not over till it is over, and kept pushing forward, ultimately turning things around and achieving success.

word_list.word: it is dogged that does it
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz dˈɑːɡᵻd ðæt dˈʌz ɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that success is achieved through hard work, perseverance, and determination, highlighting the importance of resilience and persistence in accomplishing one's goals
used to imply the more one consumes or experiences something, the more one desires it

word_list.word: difficult is done at once; (the|) impossible (only|) takes a little longer
word_list.pronunciation: dˈɪfɪkəlt ɪz dˈʌn ɐtwˈʌns ðɪ ɔːɹ ɪmpˈɑːsəbəl ˈoʊnli ɔːɹ tˈeɪks ɐ lˈɪɾəl lˈɑːŋɡɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that challenging tasks can be accomplished with effort and determination, and that seemingly impossible goals are still achievable but may require more time, patience, perseverance, and effort

word_list.pronunciation: fˈɜːst dɪzˈɜːv ænd ɔːɹ ðˈɛn dɪzˈaɪɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is important to prove oneself worthy of something through hard work, dedication, and effort, before feeling entitled to it

word_list.word: first step is always the hardest
word_list.pronunciation: fˈɜːst stˈɛp ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz ðə hˈɑːɹdəst

senses.definition: used to emphasize that starting a task or project can be difficult due to fear and uncertainty, but taking the first step is essential to building momentum and achieving success

word_list.word: he that would eat the fruit must climb the tree
word_list.phonetic_text: hi ðæt wʊd i:t ðə fru:t mʌst klaɪm ðə tri:
word_list.phonetic_am_text: hi dhāt vood it dhē froot mast klaim dhē tri
word_list.pronunciation: hiː ðæt wʊd ˈiːt ðə fɹˈuːt mˈʌst klˈaɪm ðə tɹˈiː

senses.definition: used to imply that to achieve a desired outcome or goal, one must be willing to put in the necessary effort, take risks, and face challenges

word_list.word: it is better to wear out than to rust out
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz bˈɛɾɚ tə wˈɛɹ ˈaʊt ðɐn tə ɹˈʌst ˈaʊt

senses.definition: used to imply that it is preferable to live an active and engaged life, even if it leads to exhaustion, rather than leading an idle and unfulfilling life, which can result in physical and mental decline

word_list.word: diligence is the mother of good (luck|fortune)
word_list.pronunciation: dˈɪlɪdʒəns ɪz ðə mˈʌðɚɹ ʌv ɡˈʊd lˈʌk fˈɔːɹtʃən

senses.definition: used to imply that success or good fortune is often the result of hard work, persistence, and consistent effort over time

word_list.word: where bees are, there is honey
word_list.pronunciation: wˌɛɹ bˈiːz ɑːɹ ðɛɹ ɪz hˈʌni

senses.definition: used to suggest that hard work and effort are necessary for success and that where people are actively engaged in work, there will be a favorable outcome or reward

word_list.pronunciation: wˈɜːk nˈɛvɚ hˈɜːt ˈɛnɪwˌʌn ɔːɹ ˈɛnɪbˌɑːdi

senses.definition: used to suggest that engaging in productive activity, such as work, is generally beneficial to a person's well-being and should be embraced rather than avoided

word_list.word: it is never too late to mend
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz nˈɛvɚ tˈuː lˈeɪt tə mˈɛnd

senses.definition: used to suggest that regardless of how bad a situation may seem, it is always possible to make amends or improve the situation through positive action and effort

word_list.word: keep your shop and your shop (will|shall) keep you
word_list.pronunciation: kˈiːp jʊɹ ʃˈɑːp ænd jʊɹ ʃˈɑːp wɪl ɔːɹ ʃˌæl kˈiːp juː

senses.definition: used to imply that if one works hard and diligently to maintain one's business, it will provide for one and help to achieve financial stability and success

word_list.word: in for a penny (, in for a pond|)
word_list.pronunciation: ɪn fɚɹə pˈɛni ɪn fɚɹə pˈɑːnd ɔːɹ

senses.definition: used to suggest that once someone has committed to a course of action, it is better to fully commit and take additional risk or expense rather than giving up halfway through

word_list.word: he that would eat the kernel must crack the nut

senses.definition: used to encourage people to take action and put in the necessary effort to achieve their goals, rather than simply waiting for success to come to them

word_list.word: do and undo, the day is long enough
word_list.pronunciation: dˈuː ænd ʌndˈuː ðə dˈeɪ ɪz lˈɑːŋ ɪnˈʌf

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of persistence and patience in achieving goals and not being discouraged by setbacks or mistakes

word_list.word: art is long and life is short
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɑːɹt ɪz lˈɑːŋ ænd lˈaɪf ɪz ʃˈɔːɹt

senses.definition: used to suggest that that creating or mastering a particular art or skill can be a lifelong pursuit, and that one should use their time wisely to pursue their passions and achieve their goals

examples.x: Art is long and life is short, my mentor reminded me, encouraging me to use my youth to develop the skills and knowledge I would need for a lifetime of painting.
